---
title: "Figure 6 (micro vs macro, GAUSSIAN frame) distortion lines — micro_IR | micro_R | macro_R | macro_IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Twin of figure_5_RvsIR_distortion_lines_G.Rmd, extended to the four algorithms micro_IR / micro_R /
macro_R / macro_IR. Same Gaussian frame (battery_sim_G, anchored at theta_sim -> keeps at-truth
misspecification AND the algorithm contrast). Diagonal of each distortion matrix, per parameter (rows)
x N_ch (columns), colour = algorithm, noise 0.1, x = Delta t * k_off.
95% bootstrap ribbon + 68% bar. log-log, reference 1 = no distortion.

Data:  macro from figures/data/1c2ae6f (nsim = 10000);  micro from figures/data/87889e6 (nsim 100 & 1000).
Only replicate counts >= NSIM_MIN (1000) are kept -- the nsim=100 micro runs are dropped as too noisy to
resolve a modest distortion from 1 (their 95% CI spans ~1.9x; see figure_6 notes). With the data as it
stands this leaves micro_IR at N_ch=5 only (the sole micro run at 1000); micro_R has no run >= 1000.
Replicate count -> linetype (1000 dashed / 10000 solid).

Everything that is a DATA DOMAIN is derived from the files, never fixed by hand:
  - the N_ch columns          = the N_ch present in the window [NCH_MIN, NCH_MAX];
  - the interval (x) breaks   = round decades spanning the interval_in_tau present;
  - the parameter rows        = every diagonal param_index present (param_index == param_col).
Only genuine CHOICES stay literal: the four algorithms (the request), the N_ch window, colours,
the linetype<-replicate map, and the axis LABELS (prose, not derivable). Read by column NAME only.
ONE Rmd emits THREE PDFs (Gaussian Information / Gaussian Sample / Correlation distortion).
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r params}
MACRO_DIR  <- "../data/1c2ae6f"
MICRO_DIR  <- "../data/87889e6"
NOISE      <- "0.1"
NCH_MIN    <- 5                                # requested window; the actual columns come from the data
NCH_MAX    <- 100
MACRO_NSIM <- 10000                            # macro: the 10000-replicate run
NSIM_MIN   <- 1000                             # only trust replicate counts >= 1000 (drops the nsim=100 micro runs)
REF        <- 1
LOGW_MAX   <- 1.3                              # drfigure_6_micro_macro_linearop points whose 95% CI spans > ~20x (guard; G_b is PSD)

# algorithm order requested: micro_IR, micro_R, macro_R, macro_IR (a genuine choice, not a data domain)
ALGO_SPEC <- tribble(
  ~algo,      ~dir,       ~nsim_keep,
  "micro_IR", MICRO_DIR,  NULL,               # micro: keep every replicate run (100 & 1000)
  "micro_R",  MICRO_DIR,  NULL,
  "macro_R",  MACRO_DIR,  MACRO_NSIM,
  "macro_IR", MACRO_DIR,  MACRO_NSIM)
# canonical ALGO_COL (Okabe-Ito): R green / IR blue; micro = light shade, macro = full shade
ALGO_COL  <- c("micro IR" = "#56B4E9", "micro R" = "#66C2A5",
               "macro R"  = "#009E73", "macro IR" = "#0072B2")
ALGO_LAB  <- c(micro_IR = "micro IR", micro_R = "micro R",
               macro_R  = "macro R",  macro_IR = "macro IR")
ALGO_ORD  <- unname(ALGO_LAB)                  # legend / colour order = request order
LT        <- c(`100` = "dotted", `1000` = "dashed", `10000` = "solid")  # replicate count -> linetype

# authored LABELS only (the domain -- which params appear -- is derived from the data below).
# params are on log10(natural) scale; fallback = the cleaned param_name for any unexpected index.
PLABEL_BY_IDX <- c("0" = "log[10]~k[on]", "1" = "log[10]~k[off]",
                   "2" = "log[10]~i",     "3" = "log[10]~noise",
                   "4" = "log[10]~baseline", "5" = "log[10]~N[ch]")

MATS <- list(
  list(comp = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion",
       title = "Gaussian information distortion (diagonal)", pdf = "Figure_6_micro_macro_linear_information.pdf"),
  list(comp = "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion",
       title = "Gaussian sample distortion (diagonal)",      pdf = "Figure_6_micro_macro_linear_sample.pdf"),
  list(comp = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion",
       title = "Correlation distortion (diagonal, shared frame)", pdf = "Figure_6_micro_macro_linear_correlation.pdf"))
```

```{r read}
read_one <- function(f) {
  df <- read.csv(f, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   # by NAME (skip provenance line); never by position
  df$nsim <- as.integer(sub(".*_nsim_([0-9]+)_.*", "\\1", basename(f)))
  df
}
read_algo <- function(algo, dir, nsim_keep) {
  files <- Sys.glob(file.path(dir,
    sprintf("figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_*_%s_noise_%s_battery_sim_G.csv", algo, NOISE)))
  if (!length(files)) return(NULL)
  df <- map_dfr(files, read_one)
  if (!is.null(nsim_keep)) df <- filter(df, nsim %in% nsim_keep)
  mutate(df, algo = algo)
}
bsim <- pmap_dfr(ALGO_SPEC, function(algo, dir, nsim_keep) read_algo(algo, dir, nsim_keep)) %>%
  filter(Num_ch >= NCH_MIN, Num_ch <= NCH_MAX, nsim >= NSIM_MIN)

# DERIVED data domains (not fixed by hand)
NCHS <- sort(unique(bsim$Num_ch))
message("N_ch columns (from data): ", paste(NCHS, collapse = " "))
```

```{r extractor}
per_param_diag <- function(df, comp) {
  d <- df %>%
    filter(component_path == comp, statistic == "value",
           param_index == param_col,                       # diagonal = every parameter present
           probit == "mean" |
             (probit == "quantile" & quantile_level %in% c(0.025, 0.16, 0.84, 0.975))) %>%
    mutate(q = case_when(probit == "mean"        ~ "m",
                         quantile_level == 0.025  ~ "lo95",
                         quantile_level == 0.16   ~ "lo68",
                         quantile_level == 0.84   ~ "hi68",
                         quantile_level == 0.975  ~ "hi95")) %>%
    group_by(algo, nsim, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, q) %>%  # nsim in key: micro 100/1000 kept apart
    summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = q, values_from = v) %>%
    filter(is.finite(m), is.finite(lo95), is.finite(hi95), lo95 > 0,
           log10(hi95) - log10(lo95) <= LOGW_MAX)

  pidx <- sort(unique(d$param_index))                       # parameter rows come from the data
  plab <- ifelse(as.character(pidx) %in% names(PLABEL_BY_IDX),
                 PLABEL_BY_IDX[as.character(pidx)], paste0("'", pidx, "'"))
  d %>% mutate(
    algo  = factor(ALGO_LAB[algo], levels = ALGO_ORD),
    reps  = factor(nsim, levels = names(LT)),
    param = factor(param_index, levels = pidx, labels = plab),
    Nch   = factor(paste0("N[ch] == ", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch] == ", NCHS)))
}
```

```{r plot-fn}
breaks_around1 <- function(l) {
  v <- c(.2, .3, .5, .7, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10, 30, 100)
  v[v >= min(l, na.rm = TRUE) & v <= max(l, na.rm = TRUE)]
}
x_breaks <- function(l) {                                   # round decades spanning the data range
  lo <- floor(log10(min(l, na.rm = TRUE))); hi <- ceiling(log10(max(l, na.rm = TRUE)))
  10 ^ (lo:hi)
}

plot_lines <- function(dd, title) {
  ggplot(dd, aes(interval_in_tau, m, colour = algo, fill = algo,
                 linetype = reps, group = interaction(algo, reps))) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = REF, colour = "grey70", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95), alpha = .10, colour = NA) +
    geom_linerange(aes(ymin = lo68, ymax = hi68), linewidth = .45, alpha = .7, linetype = 1) +
    geom_line(linewidth = .5) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    facet_grid(param ~ Nch, scales = "free_y",
               labeller = labeller(param = label_parsed, Nch = label_parsed)) +
    scale_x_log10(breaks = x_breaks) +
    scale_y_log10(breaks = breaks_around1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = ALGO_COL, name = NULL) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = ALGO_COL, name = NULL, guide = "none") +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = LT, name = "replicates", drop = TRUE) +
    labs(x = expression(Delta * t %.% k[off]),
         y = "distortion factor  (1 = no distortion)", title = title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top",
          strip.text = element_text(size = 8), plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))
}

build_one <- function(comp, title, pdf) {
  dd <- per_param_diag(bsim, comp)
  g  <- plot_lines(dd, title)
  ggsave(pdf, g, width = 10.5, height = 10.5)
  g
}
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 10.5, fig.height = 10.5, results = "asis"}
for (M in MATS) print(build_one(M$comp, M$title, M$pdf))
```
